stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from No 10 North
stories.summary: PM Andy Burnham announced he will base himself and senior ministers at a new northern hub of No. 10 in Manchester, with Greater Manchester Combined Authority staff recruited to the operation.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Burnham said 'no one in government loses when power is shared,' with regional mayors set to participate directly in national policy-making.

stories.title: Burnham plans to collaborate with social media creators
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is pursuing an active social media strategy as PM, planning to work with content creators — a continuation of the approach he honed as Greater Manchester Mayor.

topics.name: Burnham Gov Intelligence
topics.description: Procurement, tech/AI, spending, vendor & priority signals

stories.title: 🚦 PRIORITY/PAUSE: Early prisoner release scheme paused by Burnham for urgent review
stories.summary: Andy Burnham paused the early release scheme set for September and ordered an urgent review. No prisoners will be released until review is complete.

stories.title: 💰 SPENDING: Business rates slashed 20% for pubs, clubs and music venues
stories.summary: A 20% business rates cut for hospitality and live music venues was announced from April. Burnham called it a 'first step,' reversing the previous administration's planned hike.

stories.title: 🤖 TECH: Technology department closed, replaced with single AI Cabinet minister
stories.summary: Burnham abolished the Technology Department, placing AI responsibility with a single Cabinet minister — criticised by Kemi Badenoch as a downgrade of digital government ambitions.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, debates, legislation

stories.title: House of Commons in session — 23 July 2026
stories.summary: Parliament was in session on 23 July, with Hansard recording debates as Burnham faces his first weeks at the despatch box as Prime Minister.

stories.title: House of Lords debates — Young People and Gambling Harm (23 July 2026)
stories.summary: The Lords held a dedicated debate on gambling harm for children and young people, pressing the government on plans to strengthen protections for minors.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour party news, policy, internal politics

stories.title: Burnham shakes up Whitehall with new government departmental structure
stories.summary: Andy Burnham announced a major restructure of Whitehall departments, including abolishing the Technology Department and creating new cross-departmental mission structures.

stories.title: What will Keir Starmer do next?
stories.summary: With Burnham now PM, speculation grows about what former Labour PM Keir Starmer will do next in politics, law or international affairs.

stories.title: Badenoch criticises closing of technology department — "one guy in Cabinet" for AI
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch criticised Burnham's decision to close the Technology Department, calling it a downgrade of digital government ambitions and warning of reduced Whitehall capacity.

stories.title: Badenoch demands Burnham rule out tax rises
stories.summary: After the business rates cut, Badenoch called on Burnham to rule out tax rises more broadly, framing herself as guardian of business against Labour fiscal plans.

stories.title: Dame Maureen Lipman backs Badenoch for first time
stories.summary: Long-time Labour supporter Dame Maureen Lipman said she is backing Kemi Badenoch, calling her a 'truthful politician' who 'held the House during the Starmer years.'

topics.name: Liberal Democrats
topics.description: Lib Dem party news, Ed Davey, policy positions

topics.name: Reform UK
topics.description: Nigel Farage, Reform UK party news and announcements

stories.title: Inquiry launched into Farage poster row at anti-racism music event
stories.summary: An investigation was launched after claims Reform UK councillors tried to shut down a music event that displayed anti-racism posters featuring Nigel Farage.

stories.title: Met Police investigating Reform UK donations linked to convicted fraudster George Cottrell
stories.summary: The Metropolitan Police are investigating donations to Reform UK by Fiona Cottrell, mother of fraudster 'Posh George' who has been funding Farage. Reform told councillors to call it a 'smear.'

stories.title: Count Binface: Farage 'self-detonated' in Clacton by-election
stories.summary: Post-election commentary suggests Farage encountered significant difficulties in his own Clacton constituency seat during a by-election.

stories.title: Burnham tells Swinney indyref2 is 'off limits'
stories.summary: On his first Scotland visit as PM, Burnham told First Minister John Swinney a second independence referendum is off the table, while agreeing to 'engage' on further devolution.

stories.title: Burnham's Scotland visit hands Swinney a political opportunity
stories.summary: Burnham's decision not to speak to media during his Scotland visit gave the SNP First Minister room to press devolution demands and highlight tensions with the new government.

stories.title: Scottish Labour needs 'big changes' after Anas Sarwar exit
stories.summary: Michael Marra, widely tipped to succeed Sarwar as Scottish Labour leader, said the party needs significant changes to rebuild its position north of the border.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments, cross-department policy

stories.title: Burnham announces major Whitehall departmental restructure
stories.summary: As part of his No 10 North agenda, Burnham restructured Whitehall departments, abolishing the Technology Department and aligning government machinery with his mission-led approach.

stories.title: UK diplomatic staff withdrawn from Tehran
stories.summary: UK diplomatic staff, including the ambassador, have been withdrawn from Tehran amid rising regional tensions, signalling a significant deterioration in UK-Iran relations.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results, polls

stories.title: Clacton by-election: Farage reported to face major difficulties
stories.summary: Post-election commentary suggests Nigel Farage encountered significant difficulties in the Clacton by-election, with Count Binface saying Farage 'self-detonated' in his own seat.

stories.title: Greater Manchester mayoral election debate held
stories.summary: Candidates debated skills and growth for the city region, as the race heats up to succeed Burnham who has moved to No 10.

stories.title: House of Lords Chamber in session — 23 July 2026
stories.summary: The Lords held their regular chamber session on 23 July, with debates across a range of policy areas as Parliament heads towards summer recess.

stories.title: Lords debate: Young People and Gambling Harm
stories.summary: Peers debated government plans to reduce gambling harm among children and young people, with calls for stronger regulatory protections for minors.

stories.summary: Re:State think tank proposes ready-to-implement Civil Service reforms for Burnham's government, including 'No. 10 North' as an institutional shift. Key workstreams: Re:Imagining Whitehall and Re:Imagining the Local State.

stories.summary: The Glasgow MSP is expected to be confirmed for role in Andy Burnham's government after taking a seat in the house of Lords.

stories.summary: Sarwar will leave the Scottish Parliament and his leader role, and take up a seat in the House of Lords.

stories.summary: At this Hansard Society event on July 16, 2026, the Lord Speaker, Lord Forsyth of Drumlean, set out his thoughts on the constitutional role of the House of ...

stories.title: Work and Pensions Committee - Summary
stories.summary: Our use of cookies - UK Parliament Work and Pensions Committee Commons Select Committee - News

stories.title: Sackings, cabinet surprises and policies: Burnham's first 24 hours as PM
stories.summary: Andy Burnham sacked key Starmer allies, appointed John Healey as Chancellor in a surprise move, and announced VAT cut on electricity bills as his first major policy. The government's stated priority is cost of living.

stories.title: Whitehall officials offered move to No 10 North in Manchester
stories.summary: PM Burnham has established 'No 10 North' in Manchester as a northern devolution hub, to be overseen by Louise Haigh. Burnham promised it would be the 'nerve centre of a rewired Britain.'

stories.title: Burnham tells cabinet to tackle living costs at first meeting
stories.summary: At his first cabinet meeting, Burnham declared his would be a 'cost of living government', asking ministers to find 'all possible ways' to lower household costs and promising fiscal discipline.

topics.name: Burnham Gov Intelligence
topics.description: Procurement, tech/AI, spending, vendor & priority signals

stories.title: 🤖 AI minister role boosted but tech department axed in Burnham shake-up
stories.summary: Burnham abolished DSIT, splitting its functions across a new Office for the PM, strengthened DCMS, and a renamed Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade. AI minister Kanishka Narayan gets a Cabinet seat, but tech leaders raised alarm about the disruption.

stories.title: 🤖 Axing UK science department risks slower policymaking, say tech leaders
stories.summary: Burnham's decision to scrap DSIT alarmed UK tech leaders who fear the restructuring will slow policymaking just as AI advances speed up. A Cabinet Office-led AI task force will be established to 'unlock opportunities for growth and public sector transformation.'

stories.title: 🤖 TechMarketView: Burnham redraws the digital state
stories.summary: TechMarketView analysis notes that abolishing DSIT redistributes AI responsibility to the Cabinet Office and creates significant disruption at a time when digital innovation should be scaling. Implications for technology suppliers are significant and uncertain.

stories.title: 🤖 UK AI adoption has tripled since 2023 but barely deepened
stories.summary: ONS data shows 35% of UK businesses (10+ employees) now use AI—up from 12% in 2023—but average number of AI tools used per business has barely moved (1.4 to 1.6). Shadow AI (employees using AI without formal approval) is a growing organisational challenge.

stories.title: 💰 Burnham cuts VAT on household electricity bills
stories.summary: VAT on household electricity reduced from 5% to zero from October, saving ~£45/year per household. Funded by scrapping Starmer's digital ID scheme, but critics including the shadow chancellor questioned whether the funding was adequate.

stories.title: 🚦 Burnham to accelerate national care service timetable
stories.summary: PM Burnham plans to accelerate the timetable for rolling out a national care service, signalling it as an early priority. Further cost of living measures are expected in the coming days.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, debates, legislation

stories.title: House of Lords debates Commercial Payments Bill committee stage
stories.summary: The House of Lords debated the committee stage of the Commercial Payments Bill on Tuesday 21 July, with detailed line-by-line scrutiny of the legislation.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour party news, policy, internal politics

stories.title: Miliband promoted to Foreign Secretary amid net zero controversy
stories.summary: Ed Miliband, who faced sustained attacks over net zero policy under Starmer, has been promoted to Foreign Secretary under Burnham. Questions remain about how renewable energy planning policy will evolve under the new PM.

stories.title: Anas Sarwar to quit as Scottish Labour leader for Burnham ministerial role
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar is set to be elevated to the House of Lords and appointed as a business minister under Burnham, leaving Scottish Labour without a leader at Holyrood.

stories.title: Burnham faces defining test for the centre-left
stories.summary: Commentary examines Burnham's elevation as a test case for the international centre-left, with progressive governments in Australia and elsewhere watching closely whether his 'Manchesterism' can deliver at the national level.

stories.title: Badenoch: Burnham's plans are 'airy-fairy stuff'
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch criticised PM Burnham's cost of living plans as lacking substance or credible funding, as the Conservatives set out their opposition stall in Burnham's first days in office.

stories.title: Shadow Chancellor Stride attacks Burnham's energy bills funding
stories.summary: Conservative shadow chancellor Sir Mel Stride accused Burnham of playing 'fast and loose with the public finances' over the VAT cut on electricity bills, saying 'only one day in and already Labour are playing fast and loose.'

stories.title: Burnham's appeal to Farage voters poses challenge for Conservatives
stories.summary: Politico analysis notes Burnham has repeatedly flirted with Reform UK-leaning voters, posing a significant strategic challenge for the Conservative opposition who face pressure from both the new PM and Reform UK.

topics.name: Liberal Democrats
topics.description: Liberal Democrat party news and policy positions

topics.name: Reform UK
topics.description: Nigel Farage, Reform UK party news and announcements

stories.title: Farage stands by Reform stance amid Pride flag protests
stories.summary: Nigel Farage defended the Reform UK-run Norfolk council's decision to remove the Pride Progress flag from County Hall, as the party's local government record faces increased scrutiny.

stories.title: Farage makes low-key public appearance after period of financial scrutiny
stories.summary: After weeks of relative quiet amid questions over his personal finances, Farage made an appearance with Reform supporters. The Guardian described it as an 'aimless speech to a few mega-fans.'

stories.title: French far-right leader Bardella to address Reform UK conference in September
stories.summary: National Rally president Jordan Bardella will give a speech in English to Reform UK members at their conference in September, deepening the party's ties with European far-right movements.

stories.title: Burnham tells Swinney 'agree to disagree' on Scottish independence
stories.summary: New PM Andy Burnham called SNP First Minister John Swinney and made clear they will have to 'agree to disagree' on Scottish independence, as the SNP retained the largest party position at Holyrood despite losing seats in May's election.

stories.title: Sarwar quits as Scottish Labour leader to join Burnham's government
stories.summary: Anas Sarwar's departure from the Scottish Labour leadership to take a Lords ministerial role under Burnham creates a significant vacancy. The SNP is likely to capitalise on the ensuing Scottish Labour leadership contest.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments, cross-department policy

stories.title: Sir Chris Bryant appointed new Northern Ireland Secretary
stories.summary: Chris Bryant (MP for Rhondda and Ogmore) replaces Hilary Benn as NI Secretary in Burnham's reshuffle, bringing a new approach to Northern Ireland at a critical time for devolved finances.

stories.title: Hamish Falconer appointed UK's new Brexit minister
stories.summary: Labour MP Hamish Falconer has been appointed as the UK's new EU relations minister to lead Brexit negotiations, with the EU reset moving back to the FCDO from the Cabinet Office.

stories.title: Louise Haigh leads Cabinet Office as First Secretary of State
stories.summary: Burnham ally Louise Haigh has been appointed First Secretary of State, Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, and Cabinet Office Minister — making her the de facto deputy PM overseeing the centre of government and No 10 North.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results, polls

stories.title: House of Lords debates Commercial Payments Bill
stories.summary: The House of Lords debated the committee stage of the Commercial Payments Bill on 21 July, with detailed scrutiny of provisions affecting commercial payment terms and practices.

stories.title: Anas Sarwar to be elevated to Lords for Burnham ministerial role
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar is set to receive a life peerage so he can join Burnham's government as a business minister, marking a significant Lords appointment in the new administration.

stories.title: Key takeaways as Andy Burnham becomes PM
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has replaced Sir Keir Starmer as UK Prime Minister, becoming the seventh PM in a decade. He delivered his first Downing Street speech without a lectern, pledging to 'bring back hope' and be a 'circuit breaker' for Britain.

stories.title: Burnham promises 'circuit breaker' moment for UK as he becomes PM
stories.summary: After meeting King Charles, Burnham pledged to tackle the cost of living crisis and rebuild public trust in politics. He described his arrival in No. 10 as a moment to reset the relationship between government and the people.

stories.title: Burnham pledges to 'bring back hope' as he makes Healey chancellor
stories.summary: John Healey was appointed Chancellor in a surprise move, with Ed Miliband named Foreign Secretary. Burnham also announced VAT on household electricity will be cut from 5% to 0% from October as part of his cost of living package.

topics.name: Burnham Gov Intelligence
topics.description: Procurement, tech/AI, spending, vendor & priority signals

stories.title: 🚦 Burnham plans to restructure government AI strategy department
stories.summary: Reports indicate the new PM is planning to gut the government department leading Britain's AI strategy, signalling a major structural shift in how UK AI policy will be managed and resourced going forward.

stories.title: 🤖 UK businesses not deepening AI use as Burnham era begins
stories.summary: The FT warns that Burnham's tenure begins amid troubling signals: UK businesses are not deepening their use of AI, adding pressure on the new government's technology and digital transformation agenda.

stories.title: 💰 Burnham to remove VAT from electricity bills and set out cost of living support
stories.summary: The new PM will cut VAT on household electricity from 5% to 0% in October. Burnham has committed to sticking to current debt and spending rules, constraining his room for additional investment in public services.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, Legislation

stories.title: Burnham's policy choices reveal what kind of PM he wants to be
stories.summary: BBC analysis of Burnham's early policy signals covers housing, social care, rough sleeping and fiscal discipline. He has pledged to prevent 40,000 people from becoming homeless, a move estimated to save taxpayers £370m.

stories.title: MPs vote on Hillsborough amendment as Starmer and Burnham address Commons
stories.summary: A historic day in the House of Commons saw both outgoing PM Starmer and incoming PM Burnham address MPs. The Commons also voted on a Hillsborough amendment during the transition.

stories.title: Tuesday briefing: Inside Andy Burnham's first day
stories.summary: The Guardian's briefing covers Burnham's first 24 hours as PM, including his Downing Street speech, cabinet formation and early policy announcements as junior ministerial appointments continue.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy and internal politics

stories.title: Andy Burnham's first Labour cabinet: who's in and who's out?
stories.summary: Detailed profiles of Burnham's new cabinet team, including notable departures of Reeves and Lammy, and surprise picks. Ed Miliband described as 'a great survivor in the Labour party' in his new Foreign Secretary role.

stories.title: Who's in Andy Burnham's new Labour cabinet?
stories.summary: BBC's in-depth look at Burnham's new cabinet: John Healey as Chancellor, Ed Miliband as Foreign Secretary, Angela Rayner returns to housing, Shabana Mahmood stays as Home Secretary, Wes Streeting moves to Defence.

stories.title: Scottish Labour should be allowed to debate different ideas, says McConnell
stories.summary: Lord McConnell called for Scottish Labour to debate its relationship with the wider UK party as Burnham takes power with a devolution agenda. He raised the question hours before Burnham officially became PM.

topics.name: Conservative Party
topics.description: Conservative Party news, leadership and opposition

stories.title: Kemi declares war on net zero — targeting Tory MPs over climate
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch is sharpening the Conservatives' ideological positioning on net zero, drawing a clear line between Tory MPs who accept the climate crisis and those who do not, in a move to define the opposition's identity.

topics.name: Liberal Democrats
topics.description: Lib Dem news and policy positions

stories.title: Man, 28, charged with murder of former MP Ann Widdecombe
stories.summary: A 28-year-old man named Joshua Kerry has been charged with the murder of former Conservative MP Ann Widdecombe. Lib Dem leader Ed Davey said he was 'shocked and distressed' by the news; police are investigating a possible left-wing motivation.

stories.title: Investigation of Nigel Farage over undeclared gifts is 'kangaroo court', says Reform MP
stories.summary: Reform MP Sarah Pochin has described the parliamentary investigation into Farage for undeclared gifts as a 'kangaroo court', as the financial scandal surrounding the party leader deepens amid a series of related allegations.

stories.title: Nigel Farage says questions over his finances are part of 'coordinated pile-on'
stories.summary: Farage has dismissed the growing financial scandal as a politically motivated 'coordinated pile-on', as the Guardian's investigation into Reform's finances continues to generate pressure on the party.

topics.name: SNP
topics.description: Scottish National Party and Scottish politics

stories.title: Jack McConnell urges Scottish Labour to debate split with UK party
stories.summary: Lord McConnell urged Scottish Labour to openly debate its autonomy from the UK party, as Andy Burnham enters No. 10 promising greater devolution and decentralisation to the nations and regions.

stories.title: Douglas Alexander to remain Scottish Secretary as Burnham takes over
stories.summary: Douglas Alexander confirmed he is staying on as Scottish Secretary in Burnham's cabinet, even as a host of Starmer-era ministers departed. He said he was 'very happy' to continue in the role.

stories.title: Yes Scotland chief had no knowledge of post about missing £1.5m
stories.summary: Aamer Anwar stated that a social media post on the Yes Scotland account about missing funds of £1.5m was made without Blair Jenkins' knowledge or consent, adding to ongoing scrutiny of the independence campaign's finances.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments, cross-department policy

stories.title: Healey appointed chancellor and Miliband becomes foreign secretary as Burnham picks cabinet
stories.summary: Major reshuffle sees Rachel Reeves and David Lammy exit the cabinet. John Healey takes the Treasury, Ed Miliband the Foreign Office. Angela Rayner returns to housing, Wes Streeting moves to defence, Shabana Mahmood stays as Home Secretary.

stories.title: Andy Burnham's first Labour cabinet: who's in and who's out?
stories.summary: A comprehensive look at the full Burnham ministerial team, with profiles of key appointments. Junior ministerial appointments are expected to continue on Tuesday.

stories.title: Six highlights from Andy Burnham's first Downing Street speech
stories.summary: Analysis of Burnham's opening speech outside No. 10, including his pledges on the cost of living, national unity, and governing style as he sets out his priorities for the country.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results and polls

stories.title: Burnham cabinet reshuffle includes Lords appointments
stories.summary: As part of Burnham's government formation, Lord McConnell commented on Scottish Labour's relationship with the UK party. Burnham is expected to make further Lords appointments as he completes building his ministerial team.

stories.title: Andy Burnham becomes UK Prime Minister, replacing Keir Starmer
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has entered No. 10 as the UK's new Prime Minister after being confirmed as Labour leader following Keir Starmer's resignation last month. He is the fifth PM in four years.

stories.title: Burnham vows 'stable and responsible politics' in first Downing Street address
stories.summary: In his first speech outside No. 10, Andy Burnham said he is 'acutely conscious' of the UK's high turnover of prime ministers, promising to deliver more 'breathing room' for the country.

stories.title: Can Burnham win back Labour bases lost by Starmer?
stories.summary: Guardian data analysis reveals the fissures with Labour's traditional supporters — from leftwing voters to unions — and the political traps Burnham will need to avoid.

stories.title: MPs vote on Hillsborough amendment as Starmer and Burnham address Commons
stories.summary: The House of Commons voted on a Hillsborough-related amendment, with both outgoing PM Starmer and incoming PM Burnham addressing the chamber during the transition.

stories.title: Coming up in the Commons: 6–10 July session
stories.summary: Parliament published the schedule for the week's Commons business including Prime Minister's Questions and key legislative debates.

stories.title: Andy Burnham secures Labour leadership with landslide support of MPs
stories.summary: Burnham won the Labour leadership contest with overwhelming parliamentary support following Keir Starmer's resignation. He will now form a new cabinet this afternoon.

stories.title: Burnham to be 'bolder' but stick to Labour manifesto
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has pledged to build on the Labour manifesto commitments while adopting a bolder governing style than his predecessor Keir Starmer.

stories.title: Scrapping digital ID to bring 'laser focus' on cost of living
stories.summary: Labour's deputy leader said scrapping the digital ID scheme will allow Burnham's government to concentrate resources on tackling the cost of living crisis.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch responds to Burnham becoming Prime Minister
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch reacted to Andy Burnham's arrival in No. 10, as the Tories gear up for a new opposition battle against the incoming Labour leader.

stories.title: Liz Truss wheels out C-list ghouls for drab London CPAC event
stories.summary: Former PM Liz Truss hosted a CPAC event in London, drawing a mixed crowd of right-wing figures. The event was widely mocked in political commentary.

stories.title: Ed Davey urges Burnham to ditch first-past-the-post voting system
stories.summary: Lib Dem leader Sir Ed Davey has called on new PM Andy Burnham to commit to proportional representation, noting that Burnham has previously backed PR being on Labour's next manifesto.

stories.title: Liberal Democrats call for FA and UEFA to break away from FIFA
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats have urged the English FA and UEFA to consider exiting FIFA amid mounting governance concerns over the international football body.

stories.title: Farage admits fraudster George Cottrell paid for filming and let him use home
stories.summary: Nigel Farage has acknowledged that George Cottrell — who was convicted of fraud — paid for filming and allowed Farage use of his home before the last election, saying the support is 'totally undeclarable in every single way'.

stories.title: Clacton by-election set for 13 August — Farage to face record 34 candidates
stories.summary: Nigel Farage resigned his Clacton seat and is standing again in an August by-election, where he will face a record field of 34 candidates. Main parties are not fielding candidates against him.

stories.title: John Swinney issues stark warning to incoming PM Burnham
stories.summary: SNP leader John Swinney warned Andy Burnham not to 'repeat the mistakes of his predecessors' and called for a more constructive relationship between Westminster and Holyrood.

stories.title: Burnham rules out second Scottish independence referendum
stories.summary: Andy Burnham told around 25 Scottish Labour MPs that he will not grant a second independence referendum, a key SNP demand, deepening the constitutional standoff.

stories.title: Cooperation between UK and Scottish Government urged ahead of new PM
stories.summary: Senior figures have called for improved cooperation between Holyrood and Westminster as Burnham takes over, particularly given Scottish Labour's losses at May's Holyrood elections.

stories.title: Burnham finalising cabinet as he enters No. 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is expected to begin making cabinet appointments this afternoon after visiting King Charles III to formally accept the role of Prime Minister.

stories.title: Burnham to take 'more pragmatic' stance on North Sea oil and gas
stories.summary: The new PM is expected to adopt a change of emphasis on North Sea energy policy, taking a more pragmatic approach than Starmer's government, which could have implications for Scottish energy jobs.

stories.title: Clacton by-election scheduled for 13 August 2026
stories.summary: Nigel Farage resigned his Clacton seat and triggered a by-election after a parliamentary investigation. He is standing again in the 13 August contest and will face a record 34 opponents.

stories.title: Lord Forsyth addresses Hansard Society on Lords' constitutional role
stories.summary: The Lord Speaker, Lord Forsyth of Drumlean, set out his thoughts on the constitutional role of the House of Lords in a speech to the Hansard Society on 16 July.

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood: Immigration bill amendment to allow removal of Rochdale gang leader
stories.summary: Justice Secretary Shabana Mahmood backed an immigration bill amendment that would allow the removal of a convicted Rochdale gang leader, a measure to be debated in the Lords.

stories.title: Andy Burnham to promise to 'fix the big things' as incoming Prime Minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will be confirmed as Labour leader at a special party conference in London before taking over from Sir Keir Starmer as prime minister on Monday. He has promised to 'fix the big things' and has selected senior No 10 advisers.

stories.title: Keir Starmer makes Sadiq Khan a peer in the House of Lords
stories.summary: In one of his final acts as Prime Minister, Starmer has elevated London Mayor Sadiq Khan to the Lords, potentially opening the door for him to join the Burnham cabinet. 26 new peers were created in total.

stories.title: Starmer calls for Fifa investigation into Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
stories.summary: The outgoing Prime Minister has backed calls for Fifa to investigate Argentina players who displayed a banner asserting their country's claim to the Falkland Islands following their World Cup semi-final victory over England.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: Business of the House debate outlines remaining stages of Representation of the People Bill
stories.summary: In the House of Commons, the Business of the House debate on 16 July outlined upcoming parliamentary schedule, including remaining stages of the Representation of the People Bill set for September.

stories.title: Reform UK scrutinised in Public Bill Committee over anti-vaccine conspiracy theories
stories.summary: Parliament's Public Bill Committee session on 16 July saw Reform UK's leadership come under scrutiny, with members raising concerns about the party platforming anti-vaccine conspiracy theories at conferences.

stories.title: Political Peerages July 2026 announced by government
stories.summary: The government published the full list of political peerages for July 2026, with nominations from the Labour, Liberal Democrat, and Conservative party leaders.

stories.title: Andy Burnham confirmed as Labour leader at special conference
stories.summary: Andy Burnham pledged to be 'unashamedly Labour' as he was confirmed as the new party leader, set to enter No 10 on Monday. He promised a 'new path' for the government.

stories.title: Burnham planning summer listening tour of UK in his first weeks as PM
stories.summary: The new prime minister-in-waiting is planning to travel across the UK throughout August while MPs break for the summer, framing it as a 'listening tour' to reconnect the government with voters.

stories.title: Greens accuse Burnham of being too influenced by 'vested interests' to offer real change
stories.summary: The Green Party's Zack Polanski pushed back against the narrative that Burnham would offer a more left-leaning agenda than Starmer, accusing the incoming PM of being captured by vested interests.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch urges Burnham to back North Sea oil and gas drilling
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch, speaking during a visit to Aberdeen and hosting an oil and gas industry roundtable, said the incoming PM should back increased drilling 'if he has any common sense'.

stories.title: Liz Truss assembles host of rightwing speakers for UK's CPAC conference in London
stories.summary: Former Prime Minister Liz Truss has assembled a roster of rightwing speakers including US influencer Jack Posobiec for the UK's CPAC event in London, as she seeks to gain influence among the British right.

stories.title: Five new Liberal Democrat peers appointed to House of Lords
stories.summary: Ed Davey welcomed five new Lib Dem members of the Lords: Julia Aglionby, Hannah Kitching, Tim Leunig, Dave McCobb, and Mark Valladares, bolstering the party's presence in the upper chamber.

stories.title: Counter-terrorism police take over investigation into Ann Widdecombe's murder
stories.summary: Former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokeswoman Ann Widdecombe was found dead at her Devon home on 9 July with serious injuries. Counter-terrorism police have now taken charge of the murder investigation.

stories.title: Man arrested for threatening to shoot Nigel Farage
stories.summary: Police have arrested a man who threatened to shoot Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage, amid growing concerns over politician security following the murder of Ann Widdecombe.

stories.title: Reform UK under scrutiny in Parliament over anti-vaccine conspiracy theories
stories.summary: MPs raised concerns in a Public Bill Committee session that Reform UK leader Nigel Farage has platformed prominent anti-vaccine conspiracy theorists at party conferences, calling for accountability.

stories.title: David Davis MP accuses Nicola Sturgeon of lying to cover up Murrell embezzlement
stories.summary: Senior Conservative MP David Davis claimed Nicola Sturgeon 'stitched up' Alex Salmond to cover up her husband Peter Murrell's fraud, alleging she lied about her knowledge of the embezzlement.

stories.title: What does Andy Burnham mean for Scottish Labour?
stories.summary: With Anas Sarwar having turned on Keir Starmer, analysis of whether Burnham's elevation as the 'King of the North' could be good or bad news for Scottish Labour's electoral fortunes.

stories.title: Scottish MPs and MSPs call on Danish shipyard to stop servicing Russian tankers
stories.summary: Scottish MPs and MSPs have joined over 100 parliamentarians from across Europe to demand a Danish shipyard cease servicing tankers linked to Russia, amid ongoing concerns over sanctions evasion.

stories.title: Starmer's final political peerages list published — 26 new Lords
stories.summary: The Cabinet Office published the full political peerages list for July 2026, including 16 Labour nominees, 5 Liberal Democrats, 3 Conservatives and 2 cross-benchers, including former Cabinet Secretary Sir Chris Wormald and retired judge Sir Brian Leveson.

stories.title: Government publishes update on Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot following Chancellor's Mansion House speech
stories.summary: Following the Chancellor's Mansion House speech on 14 July, the government published an update on steps to prepare for potential further issuance of the Digital Gilt Instrument, the UK's blockchain-based government bond pilot.

stories.title: Manor Ward By-Election: Labour wins in Stafford on 16 July
stories.summary: Labour's Aaron Thurstance was elected in the Manor Ward by-election on Thursday 16 July, defeating Reform UK and Conservative candidates. Reform came second with 414 votes, Conservatives third with 237.

stories.title: Old Catton Division Norfolk County Council by-election held on 16 July
stories.summary: A Norfolk County Council by-election took place in the Old Catton division on 16 July 2026, alongside a Norfolk Police and Crime Commissioner election.

stories.title: New voting intention poll shows Conservatives at highest sustained average since April 2025
stories.summary: A new Find Out Now voting intention poll (15 July) shows the Conservatives at their highest sustained voting average since April 2025, though Labour and Reform UK remain competitive.

stories.title: Sadiq Khan among 26 new peers given seats in the Lords by outgoing PM Starmer
stories.summary: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan has been elevated to the House of Lords in one of Keir Starmer's final acts as Prime Minister. The full list includes broadcaster June Sarpong, former union chief Christina McAnea, former Cabinet Secretary Sir Chris Wormald, and judge Sir Brian Leveson.

stories.title: Five new Liberal Democrat peers join the Lords
stories.summary: The Lib Dems gain five new Lords: Julia Aglionby, Hannah Kitching, Tim Leunig, Dave McCobb, and Mark Valladares, as part of the July 2026 political peerages list.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe death prompts commentary on British political culture and Lords reform
stories.summary: The Guardian's Gaby Hinsliff wrote that Ann Widdecombe's killing should prompt Britain to ask what kind of political culture it wants, as the murder investigation enters counter-terrorism hands.

stories.title: Starmer says 'this is the end of my political journey' at final PMQs
stories.summary: Keir Starmer delivered an emotional farewell at his last Prime Minister's Questions, telling MPs it was the 'end of my political journey' and receiving a standing ovation. He is due to formally hand over to Andy Burnham on Monday 17 July.

stories.title: Andy Burnham to become UK's seventh PM in a decade on Monday
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will formally replace Keir Starmer as Prime Minister on Monday, shortly after meeting King Charles III to be asked to form the next government. He secured the Labour leadership with landslide MP support.

stories.title: Starmer defends his record at final PMQs: 'We did pull the levers'
stories.summary: Starmer offered Burnham full support and defended action on NHS waiting lists, child poverty and the economy at his final PMQs, also cheering on England's World Cup semi-final.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: Starmer's final PMQs — tributes, jokes and a standing ovation
stories.summary: MPs from across parties paid tribute to the outgoing PM at his final PMQs on 15 July, with the session marked by rare cross-party warmth. The Hillsborough amendment also cleared the Commons in Starmer's final days.

stories.title: Commons debates Iran and retail violence in final Starmer-era sessions
stories.summary: Parliament held debates on Iran and other issues on 15 July, with the Commons Chamber sitting for what may be the final time under a Starmer government. The House of Lords also questioned ministers on tackling rising violence against retail workers.

stories.title: Union backing seals Labour crown for Andy Burnham
stories.summary: Most party-linked unions confirmed they would back Burnham for the Labour leadership, sealing his path to becoming both Labour leader and Prime Minister. He secured the position with landslide MP support.

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood expected to be named Chancellor by Burnham
stories.summary: The Home Secretary is the frontrunner to become Chancellor of the Exchequer in the new Burnham government, ahead of 'risky' Ed Miliband, according to senior Labour figures.

stories.title: Burnham plays down prospect of wealth tax but says 'a little more' may be needed
stories.summary: In an interview with Gary Lineker, the incoming Labour leader distanced himself from a wealth tax but acknowledged he may need to ask taxpayers for 'a little more' to fund public services.

stories.title: Badenoch urges Burnham to condemn Labour defence investment plan
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch challenged incoming PM Andy Burnham to condemn the defence investment plan, as No 10 described the funding as 'credible'. Badenoch brought rare warmth to Starmer's final PMQs.

stories.title: Badenoch pays tribute to Starmer at final PMQs despite political rivalry
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch offered grudging respect to Starmer at his final PMQs, departing from the normally adversarial tone of the session in recognition of his departure from frontline politics.

stories.title: Ed Davey calls for everyone to 'live with security, dignity and freedom' at PMQs
stories.summary: Liberal Democrat leader Sir Ed Davey used Starmer's final PMQs to press the government on rights and security issues, reiterating the Lib Dems' core values ahead of the new Burnham administration.

stories.title: Senior Lib Dems worried about becoming 'centrist midlife party' under Burnham era
stories.summary: Senior Liberal Democrats, including those on Davey's leadership team, are concerned the party risks becoming pigeonholed as a centrist, middle-aged party as Labour shifts leftward under Burnham.

stories.title: Farage's £5m crypto gift: Bank of England governor says he would have delayed meeting
stories.summary: The Bank of England governor said he would have put off a meeting with Nigel Farage last autumn had he known about the Reform UK leader's £5m cryptocurrency gift. The donation's origins continue to attract scrutiny.

stories.title: Reform proposes £100m 'round-the-clock' security plan for Farage
stories.summary: Reform UK has proposed a £100m security package for Nigel Farage, claiming he 'lives under the ever-present threat of mortal danger'. The plan has drawn criticism as excessive.

stories.title: Man arrested over social media post threatening to shoot Farage
stories.summary: A man has been arrested in connection with an alleged social media post threatening to shoot Reform UK leader Nigel Farage, highlighting ongoing security concerns.

stories.title: SNP comments on Andy Burnham's path to No. 10
stories.summary: The SNP has raised questions about the Westminster power transition, noting that ordinary Labour party members did not get a vote in choosing Burnham as leader — a democratic process concern highlighted by SNP figures.

stories.title: Interim Housing Ombudsman appointed ahead of leadership transition
stories.summary: The government has appointed Andrea Keenoy as the new Interim Housing Ombudsman from 1 August 2026, as the outgoing ombudsman steps down on 31 July. The appointment was made ahead of the PM transition.

stories.title: Burnham expected to announce major cabinet reshuffle on Monday
stories.summary: Shabana Mahmood is the frontrunner for Chancellor, with a full cabinet reshuffle expected as Andy Burnham formally takes office. Senior Labour figures suggest several ministerial changes are planned.

stories.title: By-elections triggered for Greater Manchester mayor and Clacton
stories.summary: Andy Burnham's departure as Greater Manchester mayor to become PM will trigger a by-election for his replacement. A Clacton by-election is also upcoming, according to poll tracking reports.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch calls for proportional representation clarity from Burnham
stories.summary: An open letter to Andy Burnham calls on him to honour a commitment to proportional representation made less than two months ago, pressing the incoming PM to establish a National Commission on Electoral Reform.

stories.title: Burnham plans sweeping changes to House of Lords including elected mayors
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is reportedly planning an early announcement on devolution reforms, with one option being the appointment of indirectly elected mayors to the House of Lords — a significant constitutional change.

stories.title: Lords question government on rising violence against retail workers
stories.summary: Members of the House of Lords questioned the government on tackling the rising levels of violence against retail workers, pressing ministers for stronger protections and enforcement measures.

stories.title: Hillsborough legislation clears Commons in Starmer's final days
stories.summary: Landmark Hillsborough legislation cleared the House of Commons in the final days of the Starmer government, driven by decades of campaigning by victims' families rather than political initiative, critics noted.
